title: Extend home timeline query with followed-hashtag UNION branch
description:
## What
Extend the home timeline SQL query to include a UNION branch that pulls in public posts containing hashtags the authenticated persona follows (via post_tags and followed_hashtags tables). Deduplicate posts that appear in both the followed-accounts branch and the hashtag branch. Apply normal since_id/max_id/limit pagination across the merged result set. Only `public` visibility posts qualify via hashtag following (not `unlisted`).

## Why
Following a hashtag is useless if those posts don't appear in the home timeline. The UNION approach keeps the query set-based and avoids application-side merging of two separate queries, which is error-prone for cursor-based pagination.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read the existing home timeline SQL query in this codebase to understand its current structure, join pattern, and pagination WHERE clauses; understand how post_tags is populated when a post is created (local posts) and when a remote post is ingested (inbox handling); check how mastodon.social implements hashtag timeline inclusion (inspect its source or documented behavior)
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: rewrite home timeline query as a UNION of (existing followed-account posts query) UNION ALL (hashtag posts query WHERE visibility='public' AND hashtag IN followed_hashtags for persona AND post NOT from muted/blocked account), then apply DISTINCT by post id, order by id DESC, apply since_id/max_id/limit; ensure pagination cursors work correctly across the merged set
- Spawn a Test subagent to: follow hashtag 'testhashtag'; create a post from a non-followed account containing #testhashtag with public visibility; fetch home timeline and assert the post appears; create a post from same non-followed account with unlisted visibility and assert it does NOT appear; create a post from a followed account containing #testhashtag and assert it appears exactly once (no duplicate); mute an account and assert their hashtagged posts do not appear

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social home timeline behavior with followed hashtags — a public post from a non-followed account containing a followed hashtag appears in the home timeline; this is the behavioral ground truth
- Test approach: deterministic integration test with controlled accounts and posts; assert post presence/absence by id in timeline response; assert no duplicate ids in response; assert pagination cursors (since_id, max_id) correctly exclude/include posts at boundaries

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Only `public` visibility qualifies for hashtag-follow inclusion — not `unlisted`, not `followers_only`, not `direct`
- Posts from muted or blocked accounts must not appear even if they contain a followed hashtag
- UNION deduplication must be by snowflake id — two branches returning the same post_id must produce exactly one result row
- Pagination (since_id/max_id) must apply after the UNION merge and ordering, not separately to each branch
